title: oxalis rosea
intro: Oxalis rosea, also known as the wood-sorrel or pink wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species within the wood sorrel genus. The leaves of the Oxalis rosea plant contain oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Oxalis rosea include its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of kidney stones due to its diuretic properties. Oxalis rosea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a flavoring agent in herbal remedies.
